prompt: Could aspirin blunt hypertrophy or strength gains?
expected_output: Greg said the direct aspirin-specific hypertrophy evidence is limited, so certainty is low. However, because aspirin overlaps with anti-inflammatory pathways relevant to adaptation, he considered blunting effects plausible at higher doses in young healthy lifters. He contrasted this with context-dependent findings where anti-inflammatory effects may differ by baseline inflammation status (e.g., older vs younger populations). Practical takeaway: avoid assuming chronic high-dose aspirin is adaptation-neutral when hypertrophy is a priority.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on aspirin and hypertrophy
